title: First model counterexample executed
detail: VIP · DAMAGED · 4500 cents → REPLACEMENT; inventory 10/0 → 9/1

counterexample.title: counterexample-vip-damaged-4500
counterexample.rationale: High: it changes only customerTier relative to the observed 4500-cent case and only amountCents relative to the observed VIP case, directly separating tier-driven from amount-driven explanations with minimal redundancy.
counterexample.expectedDiscrimination: This crossed input combines VIP tier with the previously observed below-12000 amount, so the tier-driven and amount-driven hypotheses imply different decisions.

title: High-information counterexample executed
detail: STANDARD · DAMAGED · 75000 cents → MANUAL_REVIEW; inventory 10/0 → 10/0

counterexample.title: counterexample-standard-damaged-75000
counterexample.rationale: Maximum among the remaining contrasts: the prior VIP-at-low-amount trace strongly favored tier over the proposed 12000-cent split, so a STANDARD DAMAGED return at 75000 cents probes the opposite crossed cell, the unresolved high-value priority exception, rule precedence, and inventory-movement timing in one workflow without duplicating an executed input.
counterexample.expectedDiscrimination: This crossed input combines STANDARD tier with 75000 cents, materially above the 12000-cent maximum in all automatically processed traces, separating the tier rule from the high-amount rule while exposing any high-value priority exception. Using the unresolved high-value region with STANDARD tier tests whether priority handling overrides the observed tier split; before/after state and recorded side effects can also reveal whether quarantine movement occurs within the workflow or is deferred.

title: Host narrowed the exact threshold
detail: 49999 cents processes automatically; 50000 cents enters review.

title: Host priority counterexample executed
detail: VIP · DAMAGED · 50000 cents → MANUAL_REVIEW; inventory 10/0 → 10/0

counterexample.title: Host-derived counterexample · priority at the boundary
counterexample.rationale: The host crosses customer tier at the exact review boundary to distinguish threshold priority from VIP replacement.

title: Host stock-exhaustion counterexample executed
detail: VIP · DAMAGED · 12000 cents → FAILED (INSUFFICIENT_SELLABLE_STOCK); inventory 0/0 → 0/0; 0 side effects

counterexample.title: Host-derived counterexample · replacement stock exhausted
counterexample.rationale: A VIP replacement with no sellable unit must fail before a shipment, return record, or inventory mutation is created.

observedOutcome.failureMessage: replacement cannot be issued without sellable stock

title: Host bounded the covered high-value interval
detail: VIP · DAMAGED · 75000 cents → MANUAL_REVIEW; inventory 10/0 → 10/0

title: Contract rule · priority 1
detail: For an observed Web DAMAGED return with amountCents greater than or equal to 50000, apply MANUAL_REVIEW before tier-specific processing: create a PENDING_REVIEW return record, leave sellable and quarantine inventory unchanged, and return only a HIGH_VALUE review-queue side effect.

hypothesis.statement: For an observed Web DAMAGED return with amountCents greater than or equal to 50000, apply MANUAL_REVIEW before tier-specific processing: create a PENDING_REVIEW return record, leave sellable and quarantine inventory unchanged, and return only a HIGH_VALUE review-queue side effect.

title: Contract rule · priority 2
detail: For an observed Web VIP DAMAGED return below 50000 cents, replacement requires sellable stock. With sufficient stock, issue REPLACEMENT, decrement sellable by one, increment quarantine by one, and return shipment and quarantine-move side effects. With zero sellable stock, reject with INSUFFICIENT_SELLABLE_STOCK before persistence: create no return record, leave inventory unchanged, and return no shipment or other side effect.

hypothesis.statement: For an observed Web VIP DAMAGED return below 50000 cents, replacement requires sellable stock. With sufficient stock, issue REPLACEMENT, decrement sellable by one, increment quarantine by one, and return shipment and quarantine-move side effects. With zero sellable stock, reject with INSUFFICIENT_SELLABLE_STOCK before persistence: create no return record, leave inventory unchanged, and return no shipment or other side effect.

title: Contract rule · priority 3
detail: For an observed Web STANDARD DAMAGED return below 50000 cents, issue REFUND for amountCents, keep sellable unchanged, increment quarantine by one, and return refund-ledger and quarantine-move side effects.

hypothesis.statement: For an observed Web STANDARD DAMAGED return below 50000 cents, issue REFUND for amountCents, keep sellable unchanged, increment quarantine by one, and return refund-ledger and quarantine-move side effects.
